title: Custody Law and Eastern European Diplomacy

jokes.text: Well, I wanted to get the baby a doll and since the father might be Andre or Chang, I wanted all ethnic bases covered. So there's a little African-American girl.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining her baby shower gift choice
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly trying to cover all racial possibilities with dolls
jokes.explanation: Classic Annie over-thinking creating awkward racial comedy. Well-integrated with her character's tendency to over-prepare.

jokes.text: And there's a very nice letter from the manufacturer promising to expand their selection.
jokes.setup: Following up on the racial doll situation
jokes.punchline: Annie actually contacted the manufacturer about diversity
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Annie's neurotic over-preparation. The detail about contacting manufacturers is hilariously specific.

jokes.text: Oh, is everyone enjoying their egg rolls and/or Mieliepap. A traditional maize porridge popular in Ghana?
jokes.setup: Annie trying to be culturally inclusive with food
jokes.punchline: Awkward pronunciation and overly specific explanation of African food
jokes.explanation: Continues the theme of Annie's misguided cultural sensitivity, with the awkward pronunciation adding to the cringe.

jokes.text: History's greatest chump. We're talking about Joe Kennedy, right?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Joseph raising a child that wasn't his
jokes.punchline: Pierce calls biblical Joseph history's greatest chump, Jeff misunderstands as Kennedy
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion of religious sentiment with Pierce's cynicism, plus Jeff's misdirection to Kennedy. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: We're talking about Joe Kennedy, right?
jokes.setup: Following his 'greatest chump' comment
jokes.punchline: Reveals he thinks they meant Joe Kennedy, not biblical Joseph
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Pierce's confusion. Adds layer to the previous joke while maintaining his character's obliviousness.

jokes.text: It's cool. If it's mine, I'll be a lovable uncle. Okay, a creepy uncle. Final offer.
jokes.setup: Chang trying to be reassuring about potential fatherhood
jokes.punchline: Immediately downgrading from lovable to creepy uncle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang - his self-awareness about being creepy delivered as a negotiation. The 'final offer' framing is excellent.

jokes.text: When your child is a lone scavenger in the nuclear wasteland, clean water will be worth its weight in gold.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed giving a baby shower gift
jokes.punchline: Apocalyptic survivalist rationale for practical gift
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Troy and Abed's pop culture-filtered worldview. The contrast between baby shower and post-apocalyptic preparation is brilliant.

jokes.text: You know, you really shouldn't say 'you people.' / Oh, it's okay. Pierce was having trouble quitting offensive phrases cold Turkey, so we've created a bargaining system.
jokes.setup: Shirley correcting Pierce's language
jokes.punchline: Annie reveals they have a system for Pierce's offensive language
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing the group's accommodation of Pierce's issues. Sets up the next joke nicely.

jokes.text: Yeah, I really got... Jewish-personed out of that one.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining the bargaining system for offensive phrases
jokes.punchline: Pierce's attempt to sanitize an offensive phrase backfires hilariously
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Pierce moment - his attempt to be PC makes things worse. The construction 'Jewish-personed' is absurdly memorable and perfectly Pierce.

jokes.text: Oh, a cigar cutter, 'Max' magazine, and hot sauce. / It's a theme gift. / Yeah, I believe the theme is 'stuff lying around my apartment.'
jokes.setup: Chang claiming his random gifts have a theme
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dry observation about what the theme actually is
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff snark cutting through Chang's pretense. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Can I get a ni hao up in this hizzy?
jokes.setup: Chang entering the baby shower
jokes.punchline: Chang's cringeworthy attempt to blend Chinese and hip-hop slang
jokes.explanation: Very Chang behavior but relies on cringe more than clever construction. Character-appropriate but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: Why is everyone in this school obsessed with race? / White-people problems.
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about racial obsession
jokes.punchline: Chang immediately categorizing Jeff's complaint as racial
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - Chang immediately proves Jeff's point. Economical and sharp.

jokes.text: So you're not allowed to bone him. / You just guaranteed she'll bone him.
jokes.setup: Troy warning Britta not to date Lukka
jokes.punchline: Abed's meta-awareness that the warning backfires
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-awareness of story tropes. Shows his understanding of narrative logic in relationships.

jokes.text: Mmm. Mmm. Mmm. Mmm. Ugh, who am I kidding? I should be putting this right on my thighs.
jokes.setup: Shirley enjoying food at her own baby shower
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating admission about weight gain during pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Standard pregnancy weight joke. Fits Shirley's character but lacks originality.

jokes.text: Please. In my country, we give nine millimeter to little girl for sweet 16. Try grenade launcher.
jokes.setup: Video game weapon discussion
jokes.punchline: Lukka's casual reference to real-world violence
jokes.explanation: Good foreshadowing of Lukka's dark past disguised as gamer bravado. The escalation to grenade launcher works well.

jokes.text: Like, uh, Britney Spears. / Exactly like Britney Spears.
jokes.setup: Lukka trying to relate Britta's name to something familiar
jokes.punchline: Britta's deadpan agreement despite it making no sense
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Britta going along with nonsensical comparison. Not particularly strong but shows character dynamics.

jokes.text: You are her boyfriends? / I love these guys. They make funny with their mouths.
jokes.setup: Lukka asking about Troy and Abed's relationship to Britta
jokes.punchline: Lukka's endearing broken English description of their humor
jokes.explanation: Charming broken English moment. 'Make funny with their mouths' is endearingly awkward and quotable.

jokes.text: Stop rep...rimanding me, and explain why I'll ruin him.
jokes.setup: Britta getting flustered arguing with Troy and Abed
jokes.punchline: Britta catching herself repeating and making up a new word
jokes.explanation: Shows Britta's tendency toward verbal fumbles when defensive. The made-up word 'rimanding' is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Name one guy I did that with. / That one dude, tall Kyle. / Jeff.
jokes.setup: Britta challenging them to name someone she ruined
jokes.punchline: They immediately name Jeff, showing the pattern
jokes.explanation: Good use of show history. The progression from 'tall Kyle' to 'Jeff' is economical and effective.

jokes.text: Oh, you mean Jeff 'nipple play' winger?
jokes.setup: Britta defending herself about ruining Jeff
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves their point by sharing intimate detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Britta immediately proving their point. The specific detail makes it memorably cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: You should have given me one of those a long time ago. I kicked your asses.
jokes.setup: Troy offering Britta a paintball sweatshirt as bribe
jokes.punchline: Britta's indignant reminder of her past victory
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to show mythology. Shows Britta's competitive streak and past victories.

jokes.text: Let's see, uh, pieces of paper, stapled together, lot of writing. I'm afraid this is as far as I can go.
jokes.setup: Shirley asking Jeff to help with legal documents
jokes.punchline: Jeff's elaborate description of his inability to read legal documents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff - using elaborate language to mask laziness. The mock-clinical description of basic documents is very character-specific.

jokes.text: I don't see Chang as being a real hands-on baby daddy.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why Shirley shouldn't worry about Chang
jokes.punchline: The casual use of 'baby daddy' from privileged Jeff
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Jeff using colloquial language. Shows his sometimes awkward attempts to be relatable.

jokes.text: I can keep a secret. I'm from the Balkans. There are many things I try not to talk about.
jokes.setup: Lukka reassuring Britta about keeping their date secret
jokes.punchline: Ominous reference to dark secrets from his homeland
jokes.explanation: Good dark foreshadowing disguised as reassurance. Builds tension while maintaining surface-level humor.

jokes.text: Yes, I-I miss cleansing our fields and forests of the unclean people who stole my country. I miss the smell of the villages burning.
jokes.setup: Britta thinking Lukka has survivor's guilt
jokes.punchline: Lukka revealing he was actually a perpetrator of ethnic cleansing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark subversion of expectations. The audience expects trauma victim, gets war criminal. Perfectly structured reveal that changes everything.

jokes.text: Hey, Spencer, come on, man. I got woman in here! / Great. He really knows how to kill the mood.
jokes.setup: Jazz neighbor interrupting Lukka's war stories
jokes.punchline: Ironic complaint about mood-killing from someone describing genocide
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark irony - complaints about jazz 'killing the mood' after describing actual killing. The mundane complaint after horror is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You think that pool table put aluminum siding on itself?
jokes.setup: Chang defending his capability as a father figure
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur about home improvement involving a pool table
jokes.explanation: Pure Chang absurdism. The logic makes no sense but perfectly captures his delusional confidence. Highly quotable nonsense.

jokes.text: I don't Chang a lot of chicks.
jokes.setup: Chang expressing doubt about his romantic prospects
jokes.punchline: Using his own name as a verb replacement
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang wordplay with his name. Character-appropriate but not particularly sophisticated. The pun works because it's so Chang.

jokes.text: So you're saying if I get a job and my own apartment, Shirley might let me be part of my baby's life? / Yes. That's exactly what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Chang taking Jeff's sarcasm literally
jokes.punchline: Jeff doubling down on his sarcasm, which Chang misses entirely
jokes.explanation: Good interplay between Jeff's manipulation and Chang's obliviousness. Sets up Chang's transformation arc.

jokes.text: Where do you keep getting these?
jokes.setup: Chang apparently producing another saw from nowhere
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated question about Chang's saw supply
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Chang's mysterious saw supply. Simple but effective running bit that shows Jeff's growing frustration.

jokes.text: Ugh. Girls are so un-desensitized.
jokes.setup: Britta expressing horror at violent video game descriptions
jokes.punchline: Troy's matter-of-fact complaint about female sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Good Troy line - his casual misunderstanding of what's normal. The made-up word 'un-desensitized' feels authentic to his character.

jokes.text: Well, 'need' is... Is a strong word. I think Shirley would feel more comfortable if we had something in writing. However, I believe that we can handle whatever obstacle comes our way.
jokes.setup: Andre trying to downplay the need for Chang to sign rights away
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement calling Chang an 'obstacle'
jokes.explanation: Andre's diplomatic understatement of the Chang situation

jokes.text: Did you see the stock market today? Up, down... Pick a direction, huh?
jokes.setup: Chang trying to sound like a responsible adult
jokes.punchline: His completely vague, meaningless commentary on the stock market
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang fake-adult behavior. The complete lack of actual financial knowledge delivered with confidence is very Chang.

jokes.text: I can almost hear the pitter-patter of little Chinese feet across the treetops.
jokes.setup: Chang talking about his future baby
jokes.punchline: Bizarre image mixing baby footsteps with martial arts movie clichés
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Chang imagery. Combining baby anticipation with crouching tiger tropes is perfectly weird.

jokes.text: I can almost hear the pitter-patter of little Chinese feet across the treetops.
jokes.setup: Chang expressing excitement about potential fatherhood
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mixing of baby imagery with martial arts movie tropes
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Chang line mixing paternal sentiment with kung fu movie imagery. Completely nonsensical but perfectly Chang-like.

jokes.text: Why do I have to put the vcr on three Bon is on five?
jokes.setup: Chang asking Jeff parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Complete nonsense question about VCR channels
jokes.explanation: Pure Chang nonsense. The technical incompetence mixed with confident delivery is very Chang, though the structure is loose.

jokes.text: Could be pretty cute. Are they baby lions?
jokes.setup: Shirley worried about Chang dangling her baby over a lion cage
jokes.punchline: Jeff's casual interest in whether they'd be baby lions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff deadpan - missing the point entirely by focusing on irrelevant cute details. The casual tone about child endangerment is hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: Look at them run. They must die like the maggots that they are.
jokes.setup: Playing video games with Troy and Abed
jokes.punchline: Applying real-world genocide language to video game enemies
jokes.explanation: Effective blending of Lukka's war criminal background with gaming. The dehumanizing language ('maggots') reveals his mindset.

jokes.text: I'm just here for my 'trekkies do it in the final frontier' hat. That's my hat. Really? Mm-hmm. Are you sure? Yep.
jokes.setup: Britta claiming she's just retrieving her belongings
jokes.punchline: The hat is clearly Abed's, making her excuse transparent
jokes.explanation: Simple lying-to-interfere setup. The Star Trek reference fits Abed while Britta's transparent lie serves the plot.

jokes.text: Save your tears for your dog mother in hell!
jokes.setup: Lukka playing video games with excessive enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Bizarre insult combining family relations with afterlife locations
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully bizarre Lukka insult. The specificity of 'dog mother in hell' is memorably absurd and fits his character.

jokes.text: They'd be dead to me forever. Maybe we watch 'The Final Kickening' together sometime. Yes, Lukka, maybe we do. May...Be... We...Do.
jokes.setup: Abed's harsh policy about movie lending
jokes.punchline: Lukka's ominous repetition of the suggestion and Abed's uncomfortable response
jokes.explanation: Good tension building. Lukka's threatening repetition and Abed's nervous breakdown of the phrase creates effective discomfort.

jokes.text: I don't want to play anymore. I feel sick. / She's strange.
jokes.setup: Troy realizing something's wrong with Lukka's enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Lukka dismissively calling Troy strange for having normal human reactions
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - the war criminal calling the normal person strange. Shows Troy's moral compass vs. Lukka's warped perspective.

jokes.text: I don't want to play anymore. I feel sick. She's strange.
jokes.setup: Troy stopping gaming after Lukka's disturbing behavior
jokes.punchline: Lukka calling Britta 'strange' despite his own horrific commentary
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal. Troy's genuine discomfort contrasted with Lukka's lack of self-awareness about his own strangeness.

jokes.text: I don't want to play anymore. I feel sick. She's strange.
jokes.setup: Troy's reaction to the creepy exchange
jokes.punchline: Abed's oblivious assessment of Britta
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's discomfort and Abed's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: You think that pool table put aluminum siding on itself?
jokes.setup: Chang defending his capabilities again
jokes.punchline: Repeating his earlier bizarre home improvement logic
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier nonsense. Less impact the second time but shows Chang's consistency in his absurd logic.

jokes.text: It's for an extremely thorough documentary I'm making on my life. It's mostly footage of me watching dailies, but every once in a while it comes in handy.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he has security cameras in his room
jokes.punchline: Meta-documentary about watching his own documentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - completely meta and self-aware. The recursive documentary concept is brilliant and very character-specific.

jokes.text: You're inhuman. You're an inhuman person. You're a monster. Lukka's a monster for realsies.
jokes.setup: Abed condemning Britta's interference
jokes.punchline: Britta's childish 'for realsies' deflating the serious accusation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Abed's dramatic condemnation and Britta's childish language. 'For realsies' undermines her credibility perfectly.

jokes.text: He's so much worse than tall Kyle!
jokes.setup: Britta desperately trying to convince Troy and Abed about Lukka
jokes.punchline: Reference to earlier 'tall Kyle' conversation as comparison point
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows escalation - if Jeff was bad, Lukka is catastrophically worse. Links episode themes well.

jokes.text: He's so much worse than tall Kyle!
jokes.setup: Desperate attempt to convince them
jokes.punchline: References her pattern of ruining guys, suggesting genocide is worse than whatever Kyle did
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing escalation. The comparison suggests Kyle's flaws pale next to war crimes, but they don't believe her.

jokes.text: What? Why? To prove I'm a responsible dad. This proves you're a kidnapper.
jokes.setup: Chang explaining why he took kids from school
jokes.punchline: Jeff pointing out this proves the opposite of responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical reversal. Chang's complete misunderstanding of how to prove parental fitness is classic Chang thinking.

jokes.text: Our mom's name is Loquanda. / Hey, hey, that's racist. / Wait. Is your mom's name really Loquanda? / Yeah. You said you were a friend of hers. / Why didn't you know that, dumbass?
jokes.setup: Chang claiming to know the kids' mother
jokes.punchline: Chang's assumption about the name being racist backfires when it's actually correct
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - Chang's attempt to avoid racism reveals his actual ignorance. The kids calling him out adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Who's Shirley? Shirley's your mom, dumbass. Our mom's name is Loquanda.
jokes.setup: Chang confidently claiming to know the kids' mother
jokes.punchline: Kids revealing he got the wrong family entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Chang's incompetence. Not only did he kidnap kids, he kidnapped the wrong kids. The kid calling him 'dumbass' is perfect.

jokes.text: Hi, policeman, how much do you pay for anonymous tips about kidnappings?
jokes.setup: Chang in trouble for kidnapping
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to profit by turning himself in
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chang logic - trying to make money from his own crimes. The shameless opportunism and complete lack of self-preservation is perfect.

jokes.text: They're nipple guards for running. Olympic athletes use them. / Buddy, I worked at a maximum-security prison for seven years, and those are still the gayest thing I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to explain his nipple guards
jokes.punchline: Prison guard's extreme comparison point makes Jeff's explanation seem worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - if a prison guard thinks they're the 'gayest thing ever,' that's saying something. Good structure and memorable punchline.

jokes.text: Did you know you can make an ashtray using only a cigarette and a snitch's forehead?
jokes.setup: Jeff describing his prison experience
jokes.punchline: Casually violent prison craft project
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - the casual description of violence as 'arts and crafts' is perfectly absurd. Great economy and specificity.

jokes.text: Did you know you can make an ashtray using only a cigarette and a snitch's forehead?
jokes.setup: Jeff sharing his jail experience
jokes.punchline: Casually describing prison violence as arts and crafts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. The casual, educational tone applied to prison violence creates perfect cognitive dissonance. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: There's no cell on earth that can hold Chang!
jokes.setup: Chang being arrested
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top villain declaration about his escape abilities
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang grandiosity in the face of obvious failure. The comic book villain delivery is perfect for his character.

jokes.text: I mean, we learned that in that movie we saw with all those dinosaurs. Jurassic Park? No, it's complicated.
jokes.setup: Andre referencing a movie about chaos theory
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Jurassic Park for 'It's Complicated'
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke. The rom-com reference instead of the obvious sci-fi choice shows Andre's different movie preferences.

jokes.text: I guess you're right. It's impossible to keep the chaos from your lives. I mean, we learned that in that movie we saw with all those dinosaurs. / Jurassic Park? / No, it's complicated.
jokes.setup: Shirley making a movie reference about chaos
jokes.punchline: Rejecting the obvious Jurassic Park reference for mysterious alternative
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - sets up obvious reference then deflects mysteriously. 'It's complicated' is a perfect non-answer.

jokes.text: Britta, there's a difference between telling us a guy likes nipple play and telling us a guy makes hats out of babies.
jokes.setup: Troy acknowledging they were wrong about Britta
jokes.punchline: Extreme comparison between Jeff's kink and Lukka's war crimes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation callback - perfectly contrasts embarrassing sexual detail with horrific war crime. The specific 'baby hats' detail is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Was that the movie where the guy flirts with the girl online, but then it turns out that it's a fat, old woman? Oh. Sorry. It's cool. Maybe it's about catfish.
jokes.setup: Britta asking about the movie Catfish
jokes.punchline: Her insensitive description followed by Troy's awkward deflection
jokes.explanation: Standard Britta foot-in-mouth moment. Her blunt description creates discomfort, and Troy's diplomatic save shows their friendship recovery.

jokes.text: Was that the movie where the guy flirts with the girl online, but then it turns out that it's a fat, old woman? / Oh. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Britta asking about the movie Catfish
jokes.punchline: Accidentally spoiling the movie, creating awkward silence
jokes.explanation: Typical Britta moment - accidentally ruining things through oversharing. The awkward silence afterward sells the cringe.

tmdb_overview: Although Shirley and Andre don't want Chang involved with her baby, he resolves to be a good dad. Abed and Troy's new friend may be a war criminal.
insight: Absurdist escalation carries 62 jokes in a custody-plot framework that borders elite territory.
